title: How has female labor force participation changed globally since 1990?
subtitle: The global percentage of men participating in the labor force has declined slightly since the 1990s. What’s the story for women?
source: ILOSTAT. (ILOEST), Retrieved from World Development Indicators (SL.TLF.ACTI.FE.ZS, SL.TLF.ACTI.MA.ZS).
instructions: [emphasis: Click] or [emphasis: drag to draw a line] with your best guess, starting from the female labor force participation rate in 1990 on the left
encouragement: Keep going! Draw your guess all the way to 2025 on the right
done: Click the button to see your result. You can still adjust your guess
good_result: Nice work! Your guess was on average {result} percentage points off the actual female labor force participation rate, which is around 55% today
ok_result: Not bad! Your guess was on average {result} percentage points off the actual female labor force participation rate, which is around 55% today
hm_result: Hmm, that could've been better. Your guess was on average {result} percentage points off the actual female labor force participation rate , which is around 55% today
poor_result: That was poor. Your guess was on average {result} percentage points off the actual female labor force participation rate, which is around 55% today
y_axis_title: Labor force participation (% of population ages 15-64) by gender
